title: Phenol-Chloroform DNA Isolation for Environmental DNA (eDNA)
author: Spangler MA
license_note: Confirmed via CrossRef DOI metadata: dx.doi.org/10.17504/protocols.io.hnfb5bn

steps.instruction: Shred 1/2 filter membrane using a sterilized blade and forceps. Add shredded membrane to 2 mL sample tube. Store remaining membrane for future use.
Add 800 μL Buffer ATL (Qiagen) to 2 mL sample tube containing shredded filter membrane. undetermined Buffer ATL Qiagen Catalog #19076
Incubate sample tubes at 65°C for 10-15 minutes. Vortex occassionally throught incubation period. 00:15:00
Add 800 μL PCI (one phase 25:24:1, Amresco) to sample tubees. Vortex briefly to mix layers. Centrifuge for 5 minutes at 15,000 g. 00:05:00 Phenol/Chloroform/IAA Amresco Catalog #K169

Transfer 600 μL of the aqueous DNA layer (top) to a new 1.5 mL sample tube. Add 600 μL CI (24:1, Amresco). Vortex briefly to mix layers. Centrifuge for 5 minutes at 15,000 g. undetermined 00:05:00 Chloroform/IAA Amresco Catalog #X205
Transfer 400 μL of the aqueous DNA layer to a new 1.5 mL sample tube. Add 1 mL 100% ice cold ethanol. Add 16 μL 5M NaCl. Incubate overnight at -20°C. undetermined 12:00:00
Centrifuge sample tubes for 10 minutes at 15,000 g. Decant and dispose of liquid. 00:10:00

Add 100 μL 70% room temperature ethanol to DNA pellet. Vortex briefly. Centrifuge for 2 minutes at 15,000 g. Repeat for samples with high likelihood of PCR inhibition (discolored, gelatinous pellets or samples collected from wetlands with high tannins or humic acids). undetermined 00:02:00
Decant and dispose of liquid. Dry samples in a vacuum heater for 10-15 minutes. Continue to air dry until no visible liquid remains. 00:15:00
Add 100 μL 1x TE Buffer, low EDTA. Incubate at room temperature overnight. undetermined 12:00:00 TE Buffer Contributed by users
